quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 5, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Stoichiometry: Mole-Mole Calculations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Stoichiometry, Mole Ratios, Balanced Chemical Equations, Converting Moles of Reactant to Moles of Product

questions.question:
Given the equation: $2\text{Na} + \text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{NaCl}$

How many moles of NaCl are produced from 3 moles of Na?
questions.explanation: From the balanced equation, the mole ratio of Na to NaCl is 2:2, which simplifies to 1:1. Therefore, 3 moles of Na will produce 3 moles of NaCl.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $\text{CaCO}_3 \rightarrow \text{CaO} + \text{CO}_2$

How many moles of CO₂ are produced from 2 moles of CaCO₃?
questions.explanation: The balanced equation shows a 1:1 mole ratio between CaCO₃ and CO₂. Therefore, 2 moles of CaCO₃ will produce 2 moles of CO₂.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$

How many grams of H₂O are produced from 4 g of H₂?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Molar mass H₂ = 2.016 g/mol. Moles H₂ = 4 g / 2.016 g/mol = 1.984 mol H₂.
Step 2: Mole ratio H₂:H₂O = 2:2 = 1:1, so moles H₂O = 1.984 mol.
Step 3: Molar mass H₂O = 18.02 g/mol. Mass H₂O = 1.984 mol × 18.02 g/mol = 35.75 g ≈ 36 g.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $\text{N}_2 + 3\text{H}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{NH}_3$

How many grams of NH₃ are produced from 6 g of H₂?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Molar mass H₂ = 2.016 g/mol. Moles H₂ = 6 g / 2.016 g/mol = 2.976 mol H₂.
Step 2: Mole ratio H₂:NH₃ = 3:2. Moles NH₃ = 2.976 mol H₂ × (2 mol NH₃ / 3 mol H₂) = 1.984 mol NH₃.
Step 3: Molar mass NH₃ = 17.03 g/mol. Mass NH₃ = 1.984 mol × 17.03 g/mol = 33.79 g ≈ 34 g.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $2\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 3\text{O}_2$

How many grams of O₂ are produced from 245 g of KClO₃?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Molar mass KClO₃ = 39.10 + 35.45 + (3 × 16.00) = 39.10 + 35.45 + 48.00 = 122.55 g/mol. Moles KClO₃ = 245 g / 122.55 g/mol = 2.00 mol.

Step 2: Mole ratio KClO₃:O₂ = 2:3. Moles O₂ = 2.00 mol KClO₃ × (3 mol O₂ / 2 mol KClO₃) = 3.00 mol O₂.

Step 3: Molar mass O₂ = 2 × 16.00 = 32.00 g/mol. Mass O₂ = 3.00 mol × 32.00 g/mol = 96 g.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2$

How many grams of O₂ are produced from 36 g of H₂O?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Molar mass H₂O = (2 × 1.008) + 16.00 = 2.016 + 16.00 = 18.02 g/mol. Moles H₂O = 36 g / 18.02 g/mol = 2.00 mol.

Step 2: Mole ratio H₂O:O₂ = 2:1. Moles O₂ = 2.00 mol H₂O × (1 mol O₂ / 2 mol H₂O) = 1.00 mol O₂.

Step 3: Molar mass O₂ = 2 × 16.00 = 32.00 g/mol. Mass O₂ = 1.00 mol × 32.00 g/mol = 32 g.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $2\text{NH}_3 \rightarrow \text{N}_2 + 3\text{H}_2$

How many grams of H₂ are produced from 34 g of NH₃?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Molar mass NH₃ = 14.01 + (3 × 1.008) = 14.01 + 3.024 = 17.03 g/mol. Moles NH₃ = 34 g / 17.03 g/mol = 2.00 mol.

Step 2: Mole ratio NH₃:H₂ = 2:3. Moles H₂ = 2.00 mol NH₃ × (3 mol H₂ / 2 mol NH₃) = 3.00 mol H₂.

Step 3: Molar mass H₂ = 2 × 1.008 = 2.016 g/mol. Mass H₂ = 3.00 mol × 2.016 g/mol = 6.048 g ≈ 6 g.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $\text{N}_2 + 3\text{H}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{NH}_3$

How many moles of NH₃ are produced from 6 moles of H₂?
questions.explanation: The mole ratio of H₂ to NH₃ is 3:2. Moles of NH₃ = 6 mol H₂ × (2 mol NH₃ / 3 mol H₂) = 4 mol NH₃.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $2\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 3\text{O}_2$

How many moles of O₂ are produced from 6 moles of KClO₃?
questions.explanation: The mole ratio of KClO₃ to O₂ is 2:3. Moles of O₂ = 6 mol KClO₃ × (3 mol O₂ / 2 mol KClO₃) = 9 mol O₂.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $4\text{Fe} + 3\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$

How many moles of Fe₂O₃ are produced from 8 moles of Fe?
questions.explanation: The mole ratio of Fe to Fe₂O₃ is 4:2, which simplifies to 2:1. Moles of Fe₂O₃ = 8 mol Fe × (2 mol Fe₂O₃ / 4 mol Fe) = 4 mol Fe₂O₃.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $\text{H}_2 + \text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{HCl}$

How many moles of HCl are produced from 3 moles of H₂?
questions.explanation: The mole ratio of H₂ to HCl is 1:2. Moles of HCl = 3 mol H₂ × (2 mol HCl / 1 mol H₂) = 6 mol HCl.

questions.question:
Given the equation: $2\text{C}_2\text{H}_6 + 7\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 4\text{CO}_2 + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$

How many moles of CO₂ are produced from 10 moles of C₂H₆?
questions.explanation: The mole ratio of C₂H₆ to CO₂ is 2:4, which simplifies to 1:2. Moles of CO₂ = 10 mol C₂H₆ × (4 mol CO₂ / 2 mol C₂H₆) = 20 mol CO₂.

scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the mole ratios in balanced equations carefully.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on setting up the correct ratio from the coefficients.
scoreMessages.0: Please review how to use mole ratios from balanced chemical equations to convert between moles of reactants and products.
